notes: Lesson 17 orientation (act 98), 'Sliding Window — the family map', 845.52s (~14.1min) overview audio, 7 designed content cues (cue_source=orientation_visual.cues, each with label/headline/narration; timing already clean vs the MP3 so no rescale). The high-level map of the whole sliding-window family, specialized to the actual sliding-window content and using the new arrays.py idiom lib (value_row, window_bracket, pointer, value_badge, edit_note, complexity, code_line) — the array/pointer vocabulary, NOT the transformer/econ/Bayes idioms of other lessons; MathTex reserved for the complexity bounds. Running array [4,2,7,1,9,3,5,8] and string a,b,c,a,b,c,b. Two review iterations. Round-1 (author): smoke-rendered every cue with manim -s. Round-2: first -ql render exposed (a) a pointer() helper bug (rendered above-row, long-gap L collided with the headline) -> fixed helper to render below-row pointing up; (b) Cue00 overran its 98.1s window by ~1s (three per-cell Indicate flashes) -> collapsed to one whole-window flash; (c) after re-render, READ all 21 frames and caught 3 layout defects -> Cue00 re-reads badge overlapped the headline top-right (moved badge to lower-left + shortened headline to 'Brute force: re-sum every window'), Cue01 window bracket label collided with the 'one leaves' label (removed the bracket label), Cue04 corner R/L step badges overlapped the headline and the first array cell (redesigned as centered inline text counters below the row). Re-rendered chunks 00/01/04 and re-verified: all clean. Per-cue checklist (formula/label legibility+correctness, highlighted element matches the beat narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Boundary '_end' frames are sampled at the cue edge with fast keyframe seeking and can show the next cue's opening title (documented cumulative chunk-pinning artifact, same as Lessons 12/13/14); each cue's true final state was re-verified by accurate-seek extraction (cue04 settled @639s shows 'total <= 2n = O(n)' with 'not the O(n·k) of nested loops'). Final -ql 845.401s vs audio 845.52s (delta 0.119s, within tolerance). Per-cue: Cue0: brute force re-sums every window from scratch — a size-3 window slides over 8 numbers, each of 6 windows re-reads all 3 cells (re-reads counter climbs to 18), 'the middle is re-added every time' -> O(n·k). | Cue1: the insight, only the two ends change — the shared middle (2,7) carries over (emerald), one leaves (4, rose) and one enters (1, emerald); the constant edit 'subtract the one that left, add the one that entered' turns an O(k) re-sum into an O(1) edit. | Cue2: the fixed-size branch — L trails R by a constant gap pinned by k, both slide in lockstep across the array, width never changes; use it when the size is given (max sum of k, averages of k, fixed substrings). | Cue3: the variable-size branch, the window breathes — R expands greedily admitting a,b,c while legal; when the duplicate 'a' arrives the rule breaks (rose clash arc); L shrinks from the left only until legal again (window b,c,a); 'R expands greedily / L repairs only when a rule breaks'. | Cue4: amortized linearity — R sweeps left-to-right once (R steps climbs 0..7), L moves forward at most once (L steps <= n); 'each index is entered once by R and left at most once by L' so total <= 2n = O(n), not the O(n·k) of nested loops. | Cue5: when it fails — the window needs two guarantees: (1) the answer must be contiguous (skipping elements is a subsequence a window cannot represent, shown on [4,2,7,1,9] picking 4,7,9), (2) extending must move the measure one way (with negatives on [2,-5,3,-1,4] adding can shrink the sum so shrink logic misfires); moral: contiguous + monotonic -> window works, otherwise reach for another tool. | Cue6: recognition & template — trigger phrases (contiguous subarray/substring, longest/shortest/at most k, max or min over a range) + the fixed skeleton (l=0; for r in range(n): admit(r); while broken: release(l); l+=1; record(best)); two boundaries, repair the ends, record the best.
